design_lock.storage: semantic keys (dict), not numbered axes — order reorderable in docs

scope.role: file-global #!hexa strict header + ai-native { } block scope
scope.file_global_default: all strict keys on when #!hexa strict present

literal_ban.impl: parser AST walk in fn body, ~40 LOC

brace_lock.rule: first fn/if/while/for brace placement → file standard; mixing = parse error
brace_lock.ai_native_principle: system self-observes, no hardcoded K&R/Allman preference

deferred_axes.attr_naming: not now, add to hexa lint --ai-native later

tier_A_safe_rename.description: low blast radius — test files with few imports

tier_B_medium_risk.description: moderate blast — multiple refs but contained

tier_C_unsafe_without_audit.description: high blast — CLAUDE.md + hooks + PATH + memory refs
tier_C_unsafe_without_audit.why_unsafe: stage0 is the product name in this repo (the self-host boot binary), renaming breaks rebuild pipeline + ~20 memory entries + CLAUDE.md rule HX8

tier_D_arguable_false_positive.description: scanner flagged but semantic is param/size/ID, not version
tier_D_arguable_false_positive.recommendation: refine scanner with trailing-digit allowlist OR rename to matmul_256x256.hexa / griffin_330m.hexa for clarity

refinement_2026_04_17b.scanner_narrowed: F1 now flags only _v\d+ suffix (was _v\d+ OR _\d+). F2 now has is_product_name() exception for build_stage0/rebuild_stage0/hexa_stage0/build_stage1/regression_stage1.

completion_2026_04_17e_rules_2_3_4.summary: ai-native 3-rule linter live — scope + literal_ban + brace_lock
completion_2026_04_17e_rules_2_3_4.scripts: tool/ai_native_lint.hexa (~270 LOC, text-based, no parser dep)
completion_2026_04_17e_rules_2_3_4.scope_behavior: #!hexa strict header → BLOCK. Without header → warn only (grandfather existing).
completion_2026_04_17e_rules_2_3_4.brace_lock_rule: first-occurrence style (same_line vs next_line) locks file
completion_2026_04_17e_rules_2_3_4.integration: .git/hooks/pre-commit — runs linter on staged .hexa (up to 20 files/commit), blocks only on strict-header violations
known_limitations: Text-based detection misses context: e.g. `x < COUNT` where COUNT is const reference NOT evaluated — but won't false-positive since it checks for digit literal only.
char_code()-based digit checks due to stage0 `>=`/`<=` on 1-char strings returning void (repro 2026-04-17).
Brace heuristic: uses `prev char is )` or alpha as fn/if/while anchor — struct/impl body braces may false-classify; accept false-positive risk low.
Warning output from non-strict files is suppressed in pre-commit (only blocking matters). Full output: `./build/hexa_stage0 tool/ai_native_lint.hexa <file>`.

enforcement_installed.tested: F1 _v2 reject ✓, F2 product-name exception ✓, legit names pass ✓
enforcement_installed.caveat: .git/hooks/pre-commit is untracked — new clones need manual install. TODO: add tool/install_hooks.hexa to propagate.
enforcement_installed.speed: <50ms on typical commit (grep-based, no hexa interp startup)

rules.rule: stage/phase number ban: stageN, phaseN, pN, tN+
same-base duplication ban: foo.hexa + foo_impl.hexa + foo_utils.hexa → 1
case self-calibrate: first file locks snake/kebab/camel
parallel suffix ban — same as F-series for folders

phase_4_integration.title: hexa build integration — all rules in single CLI gate

non_negotiable_principles.no_hardcoded_constants: system state (sysctl, file observation) drives thresholds; only 0/1/-1 identity allowed
non_negotiable_principles.self_observation: system observes own state/code, derives rules
non_negotiable_principles.python_analog: enforce structurally at parse time, not as lint warnings
